title: Succulent Egyptian Chicken Stir-fry
description: A quick, savory stir-fry that blends classic Egyptian spices with a keto-friendly soy sauce base, delivering tender chicken and fresh vegetables in minutes.
instructions: Marinate sliced chicken in 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp ginger, 1 clove garlic, 1 tsp cumin, 1 tsp coriander, 1/2 tsp smoked paprika, and salt to taste for 5 minutes.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large skillet over medium‑high heat.
Add marinated chicken and cook until browned and cooked through, about 5–6 minutes; remove from skillet.
In the same skillet, add sliced onion and cook until translucent, 2 minutes.
Add bell pepper and zucchini slices, sauté 3–4 minutes until tender‑crisp.
Stir in cauliflower rice, cook 2 minutes until heated through.
Return chicken to skillet, pour remaining 1 tbsp soy sauce and 1 tbsp lemon juice, stir to combine.
Cook everything together for an additional 1–2 minutes, letting flavors meld.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt and pepper if needed.
Serve hot, garnished with chopped cilantro and a squeeze of fresh lemon if desired.
